content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

DAUBNEY, BANCHE, PATTERSON AND REED
WILLIAM H. DAUBNEY
NICHOLAS C. BANCHE
JOHN E. PATTERSON
KENNETH E REED
JAMES A TESTA

A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

702 FOURTH STREET
POST OFFICE BOX 390
OCEANSIDE, CALIFORNIA 92054

September 2, 1976


Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street
Suite 522
Sacramento, California  95814

Re: Nicholas C. Banche

Gentlemen:

     By letter of transmittal bearing date of June 24, 1975, the undersigned transmitted a Registration Statement, together with certain enclosures apparently in satisfaction of the provisions of Proposition 9 with regard to registration of lobbyists.

     Having recently attended a Continuing Education of the Bar Seminar concerning this rather confusing area, I am now satisfied that I was not, am not now, and probably will not be a lobbyist within the meaning of the Act in light of the "40/10" hourly requirement.

     At any rate, the two matters for which I was retained to appear before the California Coastal Zone Conservation Commission have long since been terminated, and even assuming for sake of argument, that I was a lobbyist, I am no longer lobbying.

     Accordingly, please deem my Registration, previously filed, to be withdrawn.

     In the event that there are further actions which I must take, please advise.

Sincerely,

[signature]
NICHOLAS C. BANCHE

NCB:lg

The image appears to be mostly blank or extremely faded, with only a few barely visible marks or artifacts scattered across it. I can make out what might be some very faint text or marks near the top of the page and a small mark near the bottom right, but they are too faint or unclear to transcribe with confidence.

[illegible]

At any rate, the two matters for which I was

Accordingly, please deem my Registration,

Sincerely,

NICHOLAS C. BANCHE

[The image appears to be a heavily faded or degraded photocopy of a typewritten document. Most of the text is illegible or too faint to read with confidence. There are some stamps or markings visible at the top left of the page, but the actual body text is not clear enough to transcribe accurately without extensive speculation.]

embedding.qa_text: This is a letter from the Law Offices of Daubney, Banche, Patterson and Reed to the Fair Political Practices Commission regarding Nicholas C. Banche's registration as a lobbyist. The letter states that Banche previously submitted a Registration Statement in June 1975, but after attending a Continuing Education seminar, he now believes he does not qualify as a lobbyist under Proposition 9 due to the "40/10" hourly requirement. He also notes that the two matters for which he was retained to appear before the California Coastal Zone Conservation Commission have been terminated, so he is no longer lobbying. Banche asks the Commission to deem his previous registration as withdrawn and requests further advice if there are any further actions he must take. The letter is signed by Nicholas C. Banche and includes a handwritten note at the bottom: "Called/send ofeland Termination - sent over from Firm 640 Rich." The letterhead lists the firm's partners: William H. Daubney, Nicholas C. Banche, John E. Patterson, Kenneth E. Reed, and James A. Testa. The image you provided is mostly blank, with only two small circular marks visible near the top left and right corners.
